title: senecio dasyphyllus
intro: Senecio dasyphyllus, also known as Woolly Groundsel, is a perennial herb native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its woolly leaves and stems. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Woolly Groundsel is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
